D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

Dual-Illuminant DCP Interpolation

a DNG profile stores two ColorMatrices (A & D65); the converter linearly blends them in mired space at runtime

A DNG Camera Profile stores a ColorMatrix1 (calibration illuminant 1, e.g. tungsten A at 2856 K) and a ColorMatrix2 (D65 at 6504 K) — both are 3×3 matrices mapping XYZ → camera RGB. At runtime the converter picks an interpolation weight from the scene's chromaticity, then linearly blends the two matrices in mired space.

Drag the scene CCT slider. Mired = 1 000 000 / K — A is at 350 mired, D65 at 154 mired. The displayed live matrix is w·M_A + (1−w)·M_D65. Below 2856 K the weight clamps to 1 (pure M_A); above 6504 K it clamps to 0 (pure M_D65).
